aggregate_note: Six of seven checks PASS the artifact-possibility tests. Check 7 (noise floor) fails decisively: RAOK delta-NLL of 0.083 nats is 0.57x the noise floor (0.145 nats) — below the standard error of comparing two 512-token NLL means at this sample size. The mechanism is structurally real (Tier-2 zeroing is catastrophic, hook reaches matmul, no length scaling or corpus specificity issues), but the headline number cannot be defended at 512 tokens against noise. The reported '+/- 0.016' is std across 3 seeds, not the SEM of any single delta-NLL measurement.
simplest_alternative_explanation: The three-tier scheme preserves activations adequately, but the delta-NLL signal at 512 tokens is too small to measure reliably. Permutation gap (5.61 nats) confirms the method is non-trivial; the magnitude claim (0.083 < 0.30 threshold) is below resolution at this sample size and is statistically indistinguishable from zero given per-token NLL stddev=3.27.

check1_corpus_disjointness.evidence: Calibration prompts are 200 hardcoded Python strings (REPETITIVE/TECHNICAL/PROSE/RANDOM, 50 each, max 32 tokens) embedded in rraok_rung1.py and rraok_rung2.py. Eval corpus is HuggingFace wikitext-2-raw-v1 'test' split. No path of overlap.

check2_hook_placement.decisive_number: Tier-2 zeroing produces 12.28 nat delta-NLL, confirming hook reaches matmul
check2_hook_placement.evidence: register_forward_pre_hook on model.model.layers[L].mlp returns (h_replaced,) + inputs[1:], which PyTorch uses to REPLACE the module input. Both W_gate and W_up see the same quantized tensor (B3). Check 3 (Tier-2 zero -> 12.28 nats) is independent confirmation that the hook is modifying the forward pass.

check3_tier2_zero_degeneracy.interpretation: Bulk Tier-2 channels carry massive signal. INT4 quantization preserves it; zeroing destroys the model. Mechanism is real.

check4_massive_activation_rediscovery.decisive_number: Sun et al. 2024 covers LLaMA-2/Mistral/GPT-2/Phi-2/ViT, NOT Qwen3-1.7B
check4_massive_activation_rediscovery.evidence: arXiv:2402.17762 (Sun, Chen, Kolter, Liu — COLM 2024) characterizes outlier hidden-state activations as a phenomenon class but does not study Qwen3-1.7B specifically. RAOK Tier-0 channels (per-layer, e.g. [307, 1485] for L16-25) and the CF3-Jaccard identification method are not in that paper. Phenomenon class is known; the specific Qwen3 channel pattern and the Jaccard-derived tiering are not direct rediscovery.

check5_eval_length_2k.decisive_number: 0.0712 nats at 2K vs 0.0690 nats at 512 (3% growth, sublinear)
check5_eval_length_2k.interpretation: Quantization error does not compound over 4x context length. Deployment delta-NLL at 2K is essentially identical to 512-token measurement.

check6_corpus_specificity_code.decisive_number: code mean 0.0514 nats; diff from NL 0.0315 (< 0.10)
check6_corpus_specificity_code.interpretation: Tier boundaries calibrated on hardcoded prompts generalize to held-out code. Code delta-NLL is slightly lower than NL — consistent with code's lower intrinsic dim per M-Strat.

check7_noise_floor.interpretation: Per-token NLL stddev on baseline seed 1 is 3.27 nats. By the brief's formula (stddev / sqrt(n_tokens)), the noise floor for comparing two 512-token NLL means is 0.145 nats. RAOK delta-NLL of 0.083 is BELOW this noise floor. The reported '+/- 0.016' is std across 3 seeds (chunk-to-chunk variation), not the SEM of any single delta-NLL — the latter is 9x larger.
check7_noise_floor.caveat: The brief's formula treats baseline and quantized NLLs as unpaired. In practice they are paired (same tokens), so the per-token delta-NLL stddev would be smaller. Computing this requires re-running Rung 2 with per-token delta-NLL collection (script does not save these). Even with optimistic paired-stddev assumptions, the 0.083 nat result is marginal. By the brief's stated criterion, FAIL.
check7_noise_floor.remediation: Re-run with eval window >= 8K tokens per seed (SEM drops to ~0.036 nats; ratio rises to 2.3x — still marginal) or save per-token paired delta-NLL and report SEM of differences.

what_survives: Mechanism: Tier-0/Tier-1/Tier-2 design is structurally working (check 3 + perm gap from Rung 2)
Hook correctness: end-to-end matmul replacement confirmed
Length stability: no error compounding to 2K context
Corpus generalization: works on code (lower delta-NLL than NL)
Novelty of identification method: not direct rediscovery of Sun et al.
what_collapses: Headline number (0.083 nats) is below resolution at 512 tokens. Per-token NLL stddev=3.27 makes 0.083 indistinguishable from noise. The '+/- 0.016' across-seeds figure understates uncertainty by ~9x relative to the within-seed SEM.
